blue_oceans.notes: 4.83M entrepreneurship community. YC Startup School and MasterClass offer passive consumption; no app teaches the science of startup mechanics as a daily habit. Gap: one startup science concept per day (what the Lean Startup Build-Measure-Learn loop is and how Dropbox used a fake MVP to validate before building, how product-market fit is measured using Sean Ellis's 40% rule vs. NPS vs. cohort retention curves, what unit economics means — LTV/CAC ratio thresholds that signal scalability vs. death spiral, how the Power Law in venture portfolios explains why VCs need a fund returner and most investments are expected to fail, what the difference between ARPU and ARPA is and why SaaS founders confuse them causing pricing mistakes) with streak XP, founder mastery badges (Lean Thinker, PMF Detective, Unit Economics Architect, Venture Power Law Analyst). Paul Graham essays have 50M+ reads — massive appetite for the 'why' behind startup mechanics. Zero dedicated daily habit app exists.

4.3M negotiation/sales community. Never Split the Difference (Chris Voss) sold 3M+ copies — proven massive appetite for negotiation science. No app gamifies daily negotiation science learning. Gap: one negotiation science concept per day (what tactical empathy is and why mirroring 'is that right?' produces more disclosure than questions, how the FBI discovered that 'no' is safer to pursue than 'yes' in high-stakes negotiation, what BATNA means and how to calculate your walkaway point in any deal, how anchoring psychology explains why the first number in a negotiation sets the psychological range, what Black Swans are in negotiation — unknown unknowns the other side values — and how to find them) with streak XP, deal-making mastery badges (Tactical Empathy Expert, BATNA Architect, Black Swan Hunter, Anchor Setter, Silence Weaponizer), and progression from passive Voss reader to systematic negotiation practitioner.

24M+ personal finance / business finance community. Khan Academy covers the basics but passive. No app teaches the daily science of business finance for founders and aspiring entrepreneurs. Gap: one business finance concept per day (what gross margin vs. contribution margin is and why SaaS companies optimize both differently, how to read a cash flow statement and why profitable companies go bankrupt — the cash conversion cycle, what burn rate and runway mean and how to calculate months to zero, how discounted cash flow (DCF) valuation works and why terminal value is 80% of most DCF outputs, what EBITDA normalization means in M&A due diligence and why it inflates seller prices) with streak XP, financial literacy mastery badges (Cash Flow Detective, DCF Builder, Burn Rate Sentinel, M&A Due Diligence Analyst). ICP: founders, MBA students, aspiring entrepreneurs — enormous underserved market wanting 'business finance explained simply daily'.

2.73M marketing community. 'Obviously Awesome' (April Dunford) and 'Traction' (Gabriel Weinberg) are perennial bestsellers — proven hunger for the science of marketing. No app gamifies daily marketing science learning. Gap: one marketing science concept per day (what Jobs-to-be-Done theory is and why Milkshake research revealed why customers really hire products, how positioning differs from messaging and why getting positioning wrong makes all downstream marketing fail, what the difference between product-led and sales-led growth is and when to use each, how the Bass Diffusion Model predicts how innovations spread through a market, what the hierarchy of buyer beliefs is and why addressing them in reverse order loses sales) with streak XP, growth science mastery badges (Jobs-to-be-Done Analyst, Positioning Architect, Distribution Strategist, Growth Loop Engineer). ICP: marketers, founders, product managers, growth hackers craving the 'why' behind tactics.

6.6M leadership/business community. 'Leaders Eat Last' (Simon Sinek), 'The Five Dysfunctions of a Team' (Lencioni), 'Radical Candor' (Kim Scott) are massive sellers — proven appetite for leadership science. No app teaches daily leadership science as a streak habit. Gap: one leadership science concept per day (what psychological safety is per Amy Edmondson's research and how Google's Project Aristotle confirmed it as the #1 predictor of team performance, how situational leadership theory works — directive vs. coaching vs. supporting vs. delegating based on follower development level, what the Overjustification Effect is and why rewarding people for what they love kills intrinsic motivation, how Elliot Aronson's Pratfall Effect explains why admitting mistakes makes leaders more trusted, what equity theory predicts about why people quit — perceived contribution-to-reward ratio vs. their reference group) with streak XP, leadership science mastery badges (Psychological Safety Architect, Situational Leader, Motivation Scientist, Trust Builder). ICP: managers, aspiring leaders, MBAs, team leads.

6.5M productivity community. 'Deep Work' (Cal Newport) sold 1M+ copies, 'Atomic Habits' (James Clear) sold 15M+ copies — the single largest proven appetite for productivity science in any category. All existing apps are TOOLS (Todoist, Notion, Forest). None teach the SCIENCE of why productivity methods work. Gap: one productivity science concept per day (what attention residue is — Roy Baumeister and Gloria Mark proved task-switching leaves a cognitive residue lasting 20+ minutes reducing performance on the new task, how ultradian rhythms create biological 90-minute performance peaks and why forcing focus during troughs produces 30% worse output, what implementation intentions are — Peter Gollwitzer's research shows 'when X happens I will do Y' doubles follow-through vs. pure intention, why the Zeigarnik Effect makes incomplete tasks hijack working memory and how to use it strategically, what ego depletion research means for decision scheduling — why Obama wore the same suit every day) with streak XP, cognitive performance mastery badges (Attention Residue Scientist, Ultradian Rhythm Master, Implementation Intention Architect). Massive gap — this niche produces the most passionate learners of any category.

4.2M sales community. 'The Challenger Sale', 'SPIN Selling', 'To Sell Is Human' are perennial bestsellers — massive appetite for the science of sales. No app teaches daily sales science as a gamified habit. Gap: one sales science concept per day (what the Challenger Sale research found — 57% of top performers challenge customer thinking vs. 17% of relationship builders, how SPIN Selling works — the four question types (Situation/Problem/Implication/Need-Payoff) and why Implication questions 10x close rates, what social proof mechanisms trigger buying — Cialdini's research on why 'customers like you' outperforms product specs, how loss aversion works in sales — framing what the prospect loses by NOT buying increases urgency 2.5x vs. gain framing, what the science of objection handling is — objections are information requests, not rejections) with streak XP, persuasion science mastery badges (SPIN Master, Challenger Certified, Cialdini Expert, Loss Aversion Architect). ICP: SDRs, AEs, founders selling, consultants, freelancers — anyone whose income depends on persuading others.

6.3M strategy/business community. 'Good Strategy Bad Strategy' (Rumelt), 'Playing to Win' (Lafley/Martin), 'The Innovator's Dilemma' (Christensen) — proven massive appetite for strategic thinking frameworks. No app teaches daily business strategy science as a gamified habit. Gap: one strategy science concept per day (what Richard Rumelt's strategy kernel is and why 90% of 'strategies' are actually goals with no diagnosis or policy, how Porter's Five Forces identifies industry attractiveness vs. competition and why the MOST competitive industries have the lowest returns, what the VRIO framework tests — Value, Rarity, Imitability, Organization — and why competitive advantage evaporates when any element fails, how the Innovator's Dilemma works — why disruptors attack the low end first and incumbents rationally ignore them until it's too late, what a Blue Ocean Strategy canvas does and how to identify value curve differences vs. competitors) with streak XP, strategic thinking mastery badges (Rumelt Kernel Builder, Five Forces Analyst, VRIO Assessor, Blue Ocean Cartographer). ICP: MBAs, consultants, founders, executives, strategy students.

6.4M psychology/economics community. 'Thinking Fast and Slow' (Kahneman) sold 10M+ copies — the single most validated appetite for behavioral economics science. No app gamifies daily behavioral economics learning as a streak habit. Gap: one behavioral economics concept per day (what Kahneman's System 1 vs. System 2 means and why intuition fails in statistically dense environments, how prospect theory explains why a $10 loss hurts 2.5x more than a $10 gain feels good — and what this means for pricing strategy, what the anchoring effect is and why doctors, lawyers, and real estate agents all use the first number they state as a psychological anchor, how the availability heuristic makes rare-but-memorable events (plane crashes) feel more likely than common-but-boring ones (car crashes) and how it shapes risk decisions, what mental accounting is — the psychological buckets people use for money that violate fungibility, and how subscription businesses exploit them) with streak XP, decision science mastery badges (Kahneman Scholar, Prospect Theory Analyst, Cognitive Bias Auditor, Nudge Architect). ICP: founders, marketers, product managers, economists, consultants — the business world's highest-leverage meta-skill.

3.17M product/innovation community. 'Inspired' (Marty Cagan), 'The Mom Test' (Rob Fitzpatrick), 'Sprint' (Jake Knapp, Google Ventures) are perennial bestsellers — proven appetite for innovation science. No app teaches daily product innovation science as a streak habit. Gap: one innovation science concept per day (what the Innovator's Dilemma S-curve means and why incumbents rationally miss disruptions by focusing on profitable customers, how Jobs-to-be-Done interviews work — the 5 questions that reveal the emotional and social dimensions of why people REALLY buy products, what design sprints are and why compressing a month of meetings into 5 days consistently produces better validated insights, how the Technology Adoption Lifecycle explains why crossing the chasm between early adopters and early majority requires a different value proposition, what minimum viable experiments are and why Zappos founder Nick Swinmurn validated demand before building any inventory) with streak XP, innovation mastery badges (JTBD Interviewer, Chasm Crosser, Sprint Facilitator, Disruption Pattern Reader). ICP: product managers, UX designers, startup founders, innovation consultants.
